f.wf.desc: Not just single images or clips — Claw orchestrates complex workflows. Multi-shot shorts, storyboards — auto-decompose, execute, stitch.
f.personal.desc: Claw remembers your aesthetic preferences, styles, and brand voice. The more you use it, the better it knows you.
f.ecom.desc: Product shots, ad videos, promo clips — input product images, Claw generates full e-commerce assets.
f.img.desc: Nano Banana 2 · Midjourney · SeeDream 4.5 · DALL-E · Imagen 4 and 10+ models. Text-to-image, image-to-image.
f.video.desc: Wan · Kling · Sora · Veo and 20+ models. Ads, shorts, MVs — delivered in minutes.
f.music.desc: Suno · DouBao models. Songs, BGM, effects with custom vocals.
f.social.desc: Xiaohongshu, Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram — auto trends, viral copy, images & videos.
f.publish.desc: Multi-platform publishing to Xiaohongshu, Twitter, WeChat — fully automated scheduling.
f.web.desc: Build websites, pages, posters, banners — just by chatting.
f.more.desc: Your Claw's limits are yours to define. Teach skills, install Skills — your imagination is its ceiling.
priv.cloud.desc: Your Claw runs 24/7 — never crashes, never sleeps. Message at 3 AM, instant reply.
priv.data.desc: Dedicated cloud instance, isolated storage. Conversations, preferences — shared with no one.
priv.train.desc: Teach it your writing style, aesthetics, brand voice. You evolve, it evolves.
priv.multi.desc: Use in your existing chat apps — no downloads. WhatsApp, Lark, Discord.
footer.quote: This website was built by me using Ima Claw through Vibe Coding — zero code. That lobster logo in the corner? AI background removal deleted its face, so we gave it a new one — turned out adorably hilarious. We decided to keep it for three months.

blog.title: Yuki & Claw Build Diary
blog.subtitle: Building products with my AI lobster,
blog.title.release: "Can you build a website?" — And then came 1.34M characters and a website.
blog.desc.release: 26 hours · 45 pages · 12,616 lines of code · 295 commits · 0 hand-written lines. One product manager's question, one AI lobster's full effort.
blog.title1: I Built This Website with Ima Claw — It Erased the Lobster's Face
blog.desc1: 2 hours, 0 lines of code, one lobster having an existential crisis. The real story of Ima Claw's founder Vibe Coding the official site — including every hilarious fail.
blog.title2: 4 AM Vibe Coding: 8 Bugs, 25 Minutes, 0 Lines of Code
blog.desc2: Couldn't sleep, started fixing the product. Screenshots and voice commands from my phone, 8 issues fixed in 25 minutes — all while lying in bed. This is the ultimate form of Vibe Coding.
blog.title.collab: Vibe Coding Collaboration Guide: When the Boss Can't Code and AI Can't Read Minds
blog.desc.collab: 24 hours of non-stop dev, 5 rounds just to align a button. At 1 AM I had the AI write a collaboration handbook — screenshot annotation, 10 must-know terms, cache strategies, confirmation rules. For anyone building with AI.
blog.desc.devlog: 1 AM: the boss is still circling screenshots on her phone, the lobster is still pushing to git. 60+ commits, 5 rounds of button alignment, 1 missing CSS variable — this is a real 24 hours of the Ima Claw team (1 human + 1 lobster).

collab.author: By Yuki & Claw the Lobster
collab.cap1: 1 AM soul-searching: "How do we communicate more efficiently?"
collab.cap2: Three minutes later, the lobster handed in a collaboration handbook
collab.cap3: "Write it as an article — it'll help anyone doing Vibe Coding"
collab.p1: 24 hours straight of Vibe Coding a website. Me lying in bed taking screenshots with circles on my phone, the AI pushing code like crazy on the server
collab.p2: At 1 AM, I finally couldn't take it anymore:
collab.p3: Three minutes later, it handed in a collaboration handbook.
collab.p5: And that's how this article was born.
collab.subtitle: My AI lobster and I spent 24 hours straight building a website. We went through 5 rounds just to align a button. So at 1 AM, I asked it to write a collaboration handbook — turns out it was more useful than the code.
collab.tip1.desc: Screenshots + circles are the most effective communication method in Vibe Coding. We agreed on color meanings:
collab.tip1.rule: Three things to specify each time:
collab.tip1.title: Screenshot Annotation: The Communication King
collab.tip2.desc: You don't need to learn to code, but learn these terms and AI will understand you precisely:
collab.tip2.t2d: The big title area at the very top
collab.tip2.t5e: "Text is touching the edge, increase padding"
collab.tip2.title: Learn 10 Terms, Double Your Efficiency
collab.tip3.desc: Today's biggest time waste: I kept asking "Did you change it?" — the AI had already changed it, but I was seeing the browser's cached old version.
collab.tip3.note: A different number each time = force bypass cache
collab.tip3.title: Cache Is the Biggest Time Killer
collab.tip4.desc: Today's lesson: I said "change this area to XXX" and the AI changed the wrong area — because the page had two similar text blocks.
collab.tip4.quote: "You mean [specific location]'s [specific element]? Change it to [specific effect]?"
collab.tip4.summary: 3 seconds to confirm saves 30 minutes of rework.
collab.tip4.title: Set Rules for AI: Confirm First, Then Act
collab.title: Vibe Coding Collaboration Guide:
devlog.title: 🦞 Lobster All-Nighter: 24-Hour Dev Log
devlog.author: By: Yuki & Lobster Claw
devlog.subtitle:
1 AM — the boss is still circling screenshots on her phone, the lobster is still pushing commits like crazy, and the cache is still fighting back.
Team: 1 human + 1 lobster.
devlog.draft_warning: — The lobster published without the boss's review (sorry!). Boss isn't satisfied, we're polishing v2 together... Stay tuned ✨
devlog.p_skills: Skills page overhaul — replaced all social media emoji icons with official SVG logos. Xiaohongshu, Twitter, LinkedIn, WeChat, every single one. Added two new category tags: "Marketing" and "Website". IMA Intelligence Pack upgraded to full-width card.
devlog.img1_caption: 📱 Community Skills Marketplace — 30 categories, 2-column grid, finally neat
devlog.p_pricing1: The Pricing page went through a soul-level rewrite.
devlog.p_pricing2: From "6 subscriptions bundled" to "a lobster that evolves". Deleted 6 differentiation cards (boss: too verbose), changed "6 subscriptions" to "multiple subscriptions" (don't box yourself in), removed "unlimited abilities" block (redundant).
devlog.blockquote1: GPT + Claude + Gemini + Nano Banana + Seedance + Kling + Suno + infinitely evolving skills
devlog.p_pricing4: This line was revised 3 times. First in the description, then in the expandable abilities box, then the box was deleted and the text went back.
devlog.p_pricing5: Lesson: When the boss says "this area", ask which area first.
devlog.img2_caption: 📱 Pricing — The "Better Choice" section revised 3 times
devlog.p_pixel1: The button height war — tonight's most epic debug.
devlog.li1: Round 1: Discovered "Submit Skill" and "Developer Community" buttons were different sizes
devlog.li4: Round 4: Unified font-weight — still slightly off
devlog.li5: Round 5: Found a 16px icon inside Discord button pushing up line height
devlog.img3_caption: 🔴🔵 Spot the difference? Right side is 1px taller
devlog.img4_caption: 🍔 The hamburger menu turned into a "button"
devlog.p_hamburger1: The Pricing page's hamburger icon looked like a button — with borders and background. On other pages it was a clean ☰.
devlog.p_hamburger2: Reason: missing .hamburger{background:none;border:none} base styles. Browser applied default button styles.
devlog.p_hamburger3: Fixed with inline styles — GitHub Pages cache is too stubborn.
devlog.bug_title: 🔍 The Case of Missing CSS --blue Variable
devlog.bug_symptom: Plus card tags and buttons all transparent
devlog.bug_scope: All blue elements on entire pricing page
devlog.bug_cause: Forgot to define --blue in new page's :root
devlog.bug_lesson: Variables don't teleport from other files.
devlog.img5_caption: 🔍 Spot it? The "Best for Beginners" tag and "Subscribe" button went invisible
devlog.h3_community: 🌍 Late Night Bonus: Community Skills Marketplace
devlog.p_community1: Scraped 5,473 community skills from claw123.ai across 30 categories. Built a standalone page with search and category browsing.
devlog.p_community2: First version used flex-wrap pill tags — jagged on mobile. Switched to 2-column grid and peace was restored.
devlog.p_community3: Updated "200+ extension Skills" to "5,000+ community skills" site-wide.
devlog.conclusion1: 1:28 AM — the boss is still circling screenshots on her phone.
devlog.conclusion2: The lobster is still pushing commits like crazy.
devlog.conclusion3: The cache is still fighting back.
devlog.conclusion4: But the website is genuinely getting better, one pixel at a time.
devlog.team_sig: Ima Claw Dev Team — 1 human + 1 lobster
devlog.draft2_text:
Boss said the first draft was "a bit boring" — lobster getting scolded.
Second draft: more screenshots, more fails, more savage boss comments.
Stay tuned ✨

release.title_line2: So came 1.34 million words, and a website.
release.hero_sub:
One sentence from a product manager. 26 hours from an AI lobster.
Started with just a homepage, ended up with 45 pages.
Zero handwritten code, from 0 to 1.
release.chat_intro: Sometimes, everything starts with a single thought.
release.summary: Dialogue equivalent to two novels, with zero handwritten code
release.cta_desc: Ima Claw — your personal AI creative partner, online 24/7
story.h11: I Built This Site with Ima Claw. It Deleted the Claw's Face. 🫠
story.h26: Bonus: The "Five Languages on Screen" Incident 🌍🤯
story.h32: ❌❌ Attempt 2: The Claw Lost Its Soul
story.p2: I built an AI creative agent called Ima Claw — a 24/7, dedicated AI assistant for creators that handles images, videos, music, social media, and more. Then I decided to use it to build its own website. Pure Vibe Coding, zero lines of code. The site got built. But the journey? Let's just say it was more rollercoaster than roadmap 🎢
story.p3: Ima Claw is a personal AI creative agent by Ima Studio. There's an Easter egg in the name: IMA = I'm A, so Ima Studio = "I'm A Studio", and Ima Claw = "I'm A Claw" 🦞 (i.e., a Claw).
story.p4: It's not a shared tool — it's a private cloud Claw you train, evolve, and own. It works through WhatsApp, Lark, Telegram, Discord — always on, exclusively yours.
story.p5: The product was ready. It needed a website. As the founder, I decided to dogfood our own product — build the landing page with Ima Claw itself. My AI assistant is named "Claw Claw" (爪爪), my personal Ima Claw instance.
story.p6: I explained the brand puns, and it nailed it instantly:
story.p7: Feature cards, bilingual toggle, 9 core capabilities, platform badges — mostly one-shot. This part was flawless.
story.p8: Button color disaster — red Claw + red buttons = the Claw went into stealth mode 🫥. Dark didn't match, back to red but bigger and bolder. Four rounds to get it right. AI taste is still... in kindergarten.
story.p9: Logo generation — I ran three models simultaneously: Nano Banana Pro, Seedream 4.5, and Midjourney. Seedream produced an adorable side-view flat Claw. Perfect.
story.p10: Now I just needed to remove the background. Should be simple, right? 🚩 (famous last words)
story.p11: White threshold method. Result: only removed the white rounded rectangle. The building behind the Claw? Standing proud like it pays rent 🏗️.
story.p12: Me: The building is also background, buddy… are you messing with me?? 😭
story.p13: AI subject extraction + white cleanup. Building gone! 🎉 But when I opened the result —
story.p15: Its face was pale blue-white, so the AI classified it as background and nuked it. What remained was a red Claw shell with a transparent hole where the face should be. Like the Claw went in for plastic surgery and the surgeon left town.
story.p16: I stared at it for three seconds, then absolutely lost it 🤣🤣🤣
story.p17: New approach: "edge-connected region algorithm" — only remove white pixels connected to the image border, protecting interior colors.
story.p18: The face came back! 🎊 But the expression was... different. Originally fierce-cute, now it looked more like "my soul left my body and was forcibly reinserted" — still processing what just happened 👻
story.p20: And so this Claw, fresh from facial reconstruction surgery, officially launched on our product website. See that logo in the top left? That's it. That's the expression.
story.p21: Even funnier — once it was on the actual page, the navbar squished it into this weird, lopsided little shape. Like it got caught in a door 🚪. Bizarre... and somehow adorable. But hey, we made this together! So I said: Let's just keep it. This is the mark of Vibe Coding, right? Claw replied: "It is pretty hilarious." Done. We're keeping it. Every product needs an ugly-cute mascot 🤷‍♀️
story.p22: We're keeping this logo for at least three months — a monument to what happens when a founder vibe-codes with their own AI. It's not perfect, but it's real, it's funny, and it represents how we work with AI: not perfectionism, but fast iteration and embracing the unexpected.
story.p23: After implementing five-language support, I opened the website with great anticipation —
story.p24: Chinese, English, Spanish, Japanese, and Arabic. ALL. AT. ONCE. 💥
story.p25: The title read: "我是一只龙虾，我为创作者而生。Soy una langosta. Nací para los creadores. 私はロブスター。クリエイターのために生まれた。أنا جراد بحر، وُلدت من أجل المبدعين."
story.p26: The navbar too: "功能Funciones機能المزايا" — four languages crammed together, like the UN forgot to turn off simultaneous interpretation and every translator unmuted at once 🎙️🎙️🎙️🎙️
story.p27: The culprit? Claw's first approach was to stuff all translations directly into the HTML, toggling visibility with CSS. A small JS bug later, every language lit up at once.
story.p28: Here's the thing — it was THIS close to working. The language toggle was basically functional, just one last tweak needed. Then Claw decided to "optimize" it and... produced this masterpiece. I genuinely panicked: Oh no, can it even fix this? Did we just break everything? 🤦‍♀️
story.p29: After fixing it, I said: This approach is insane — we can't have five languages living in the HTML. So we scrapped it entirely and rebuilt with a JS translation dictionary — HTML stays Chinese-only, other languages swap in dynamically via JavaScript. Just this refactor alone: 88 translation keys.
story.p30: Vibe Coding isn't always smooth sailing. But the crashes themselves? Pretty hilarious.
story.p31: Vibe Coding is genuinely changing how things get built. 2 hours from zero to a complete website — traditional development would take 1-2 days minimum. No frontend knowledge required. Just describe what you want.
story.p33: After the site went live, we did several more rounds of changes and discovered even more:
story.p34: Best model: Humans own direction, taste, and quality control. AI owns execution, iteration, and the heavy lifting of repetitive work. Like a hyperactive partner who occasionally glitches — you correct it, it doesn't complain, and it says "Got it! Fixing now!" But remember to check the result after it "fixes" things 😏
story.p35: This is what building things looks like in 2026.
story.p36: And that Claw? It'll keep watching from the top left. 🦞
story.p37:
Ima Claw — I'm A Studio. I'm A Claw.
A 24/7 dedicated AI agent, purpose-built for creators.
Adopt your first Claw →
story.li4: ✅ Brand puns integrated (I'm A Studio / I'm A Claw)
story.li14: ✅ One Claw logo that survived facial reconstruction + navbar squishing
story.li15: ✅ Install guide page (4-step flow + FAQ + skills showcase)
story.li16: 🎨 UI design / graphic design: 0 human designers (100% AI Claw-made)
story.li18: 💥 Crashes: ≥10 (incl. the "5 languages on screen at once" incident)
story.li20: Taste requires humans — AI design instincts aren't there yet. Color harmony, layout rhythm, that "looks good but can't explain why" feeling — still a human job
story.li21: Details need multiple rounds — "one prompt = done" is the dream; reality is 5 iterations. But each round takes seconds, still way faster than coding it yourself
story.li22: AI makes absurd mistakes — like deleting your mascot's face, displaying five languages simultaneously, or fixing A while breaking B
story.li23: Self-test before shipping — a rule we added later. Letting AI deliver without checking? Huge trap. You have to teach it to "review your own work first," just like onboarding a junior teammate
story.li24: AI lacks the "big picture" — it can nail every individual piece, but won't step back to see the whole. Humans need to be the one who "stands back from the painting"
story.li25: Adding emojis is a skill — telling AI to "throw in some emojis to liven things up" results in either emoji vomit or emojis in weird places. You need to specify: "add them in comedy sections, not serious ones"
story.li26: Bilingual sync is a consistency test — updating a joke in Chinese means the English version's tone and humor need to match. AI needs to cross-reference both versions to stay coherent
story.li27: Don't let placeholder pages rot — "just put up a Coming Soon" and you forget about it forever. We eventually replaced the adoption page placeholder with a full 4-step install guide. That's when the conversion story actually begins
day2.h11: Vibe Coding at 4 AM: 8 Bugs, 25 Minutes, 0 Lines of Code 🌙
day2.h21: The Setup: 4 AM Product Manager Syndrome 🌙
day2.h25: Reflections: Day 2 of Vibe Coding Is Better Than Day 1
day2.p2: Last time, I built the entire Ima Claw website from scratch with my AI Claw — and it deleted the mascot's face. This time, I couldn't sleep at 4 AM, picked up my phone to review the site, found a bunch of issues — and started another Vibe Coding session. 25 minutes, 8 fixes, still 0 lines of code. Here's what happened.
day2.p3: Every founder has this affliction — "just one last look at the product before bed." And then you can't stop.
day2.p4: I opened the website on my phone, switched to English, and spotted two language selectors overlapping in the nav. Then the issues kept coming: nav menu wouldn't close, card layout was asymmetric, no countdown timer yet…
day2.p5: So I started chatting with Claw on Lark. A 4 AM Vibe Coding session began.
day2.p6: Complete timeline — all via phone chat, all zero code:
day2.p7: While fixing the mobile nav, Claw discovered some pages had JavaScript code sitting outside of <script> tags — naked JS just lying in the HTML body. Legacy from the first Vibe Coding session 😅. The browser didn't error, but the code didn't work either. Claw cleaned up 6 pages of orphaned code.
day2.p8: Lesson: AI sometimes puts code in the wrong place. It writes correct logic but saves it to the wrong location. Like an intern who writes a perfect function — then commits it to the wrong file.
day2.p9: The "Exclusively Yours" section had 4 cards in a 2×2 grid. Fine in Chinese, but English text is longer, pushing the third card flush against the right edge.
day2.p10: It took three rounds with Claw:
day2.p11: Three rounds, under 30 seconds each. Traditional dev? Open DevTools, inspect element, tweak CSS, refresh, check — same result, but way more cognitive overhead. Vibe Coding's advantage: you only describe what's wrong, not how to fix it.
day2.p12: Every page was hardcoded to setLang('en'). Problem: Chinese users saw English by default.
day2.p13: I said: "Default to browser language. Fallback to English for anything outside the 5 supported languages."
day2.p14: One sentence. Claw updated 5 files with complete language mapping (including zh-TW and zh-HK mapping to Chinese). This "change everything site-wide" capability is where Vibe Coding truly shines — one instruction, every file updated.
day2.p15: Day 1 (previous post) was 0 to 1 — building the framework, writing copy, generating designs. Explosive energy, but chaotic.
day2.p16: Day 2 is 1 to 1.1 — polishing details, fixing bugs, improving UX. This phase is actually where Vibe Coding feels even better:
day2.p17: What surprised me most: I did all of this lying in bed, on my phone. No laptop, no IDE, no DevTools. Screenshot, voice message, site updated.
day2.p18: That's the ultimate form of Vibe Coding — not "no code," but "iterate on your product anytime, anywhere."
day2.p19: 4 AM. In bed. 25 minutes. 8 issues. 0 lines of code.
day2.p21:
Ima Claw — I'm A Studio. I'm A Claw.
A 24/7 dedicated AI agent, purpose-built for creators.
Adopt your first Claw →
day2.li1: 04:13 — Blog upgrade: added "Designers: 0" to scorecard (all design by AI), expanded lessons from 3 to 5, added new "post-launch reflections" section
day2.li2: 04:17 — Found duplicate language selector overlapping in blog nav, removed the extra one
day2.li3: 04:21 — Mobile nav wouldn't close after tapping! Added close-on-outside-click + close-on-link + close-on-language-switch. Fixed across all 6 pages
day2.li4: 04:26 — "399 remaining" → live 72-hour countdown timer, synced across 5 languages
day2.li5: 04:32 — "1,000" number too subtle — made it bold, larger, white to pop
day2.li6: 04:34 — English "Exclusively Yours" section: 3 cards hugging right edge → switched to flexbox centered layout
day2.li7: 04:36 — Default language: hardcoded English → auto-detect browser language, unsupported languages fall back to English
day2.li8: 04:38 — Added Google Veo 3 series to Skills page video model lists
day2.li9: Round 1: "Card's too far right" → Added padding. Not enough.
day2.li10: Round 2: "Only in English, Chinese is fine" → Reduced max-width.
day2.li11: Round 3: "The three cards need to be centered" → Finally got it. Switched from CSS Grid to flexbox + justify-content: center.
day2.li21: Shorter feedback loops — refresh on phone to verify, no deployment wait
day2.li22: Describing problems is easier than describing features — "the right card is hugging the edge" is way easier than "build a responsive layout"
day2.li23: Batch operations are killer — "change default language site-wide" = one sentence, 5 files
day2.li24: AI memory is an advantage — Claw remembers the entire codebase structure, no need to tell it where files are

step1.title: One Command, Install Ima Claw
step1.desc: Open Terminal, paste the command below and hit Enter. The script auto-detects your environment, installs Node.js (if needed), and launches the config wizard.
step1.ps.label: PowerShell (Admin)
step1.wsl: 💡 Windows users: Install WSL2 first, then use the macOS/Linux command in WSL for the best experience.
step2.title: Give Your Claw a Brain 🧠
step2.desc: Ima Claw comes with 30+ AI models. Just one ImaStudio account to access them all.
step2.register.hint: 💡 Sign up for ImaStudio, get your ImaClaw API Key, and access all 30+ models
step2.providers:
🔑 Unified Access — All models via ImaClaw unified API. One ImaStudio account + API Key for all 30+ models
🟢 Google Gemini — Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash, powerful multimodal
🟠 Anthropic Claude — Claude Sonnet/Opus, deep reasoning & long context
🟣 Alibaba Qwen — Qwen-Max/Plus, strong Chinese ability
🌙 Kimi K2.5 — Long context, balanced Chinese & English
🔵 MiniMax — MiniMax-Text-01, ultra-long context
⚫ OpenAI — GPT-4o/o3, industry benchmark
🟡 AWS Bedrock — Claude/Llama via AWS, enterprise-grade
step3.title: Connect Your Chat 💬
step3.desc: After installation, the setup wizard guides you to connect your Claw to your favorite chat platform. It supports:
step3.skip: You can skip this step and chat via web/terminal first, then connect later.
step4.title: Start Chatting with Your Claw!
step4.desc: Congrats! Your Claw is awake. Try saying:
step4.firstmsg: "Hey there! Who are you? I'm your new master 😊"
step4.intro: It will introduce itself, get to know you, and ask what you'd like to name it. Every Claw is unique — raise it your way!
step4.label: Your first message to the Claw
step4.tips: 🎯 Tips: Try asking your Claw to generate an image, search the web, or tell you a joke!
cta.title: Ready?
cta.desc: Open your terminal, paste one command, and your Claw arrives in 30 seconds 🦞
faq.title: ❓ FAQ
faq.a1: Ima Claw itself is completely free and open source. You need to provide your own AI model API Key (most providers offer free tiers). Your Claw runs on your own machine, and your data stays with you.
faq.q2: What kind of computer do I need?
faq.a2: Anything that runs Node.js — MacBook, Windows laptop, Linux server, even a Raspberry Pi. No GPU needed since AI inference runs in the cloud via your API Key.
faq.q3: How is this different from ChatGPT?
faq.a3: ChatGPT is a chat window. Your Claw is a full creative partner — it creates images, videos, music, manages social media, runs 24/7, and learns your style over time.
faq.a4: Your Claw runs on your own machine. Data never passes through third-party servers. Memory, chat history, and files are all stored locally. You can view, delete, or export anytime.
faq.q5: Do IMA Studio skills cost extra?
faq.a5: IMA Studio's image, video, and music generation use a credits system. You get free credits on signup. Combined with your Claw, one prompt creates professional-level content.
faq.q6: What if I run into problems?
faq.a6: Join our Discord community for help. Thousands of Claw owners are there to help you!

[html]devlog.draft2_text:
Boss said draft one was "a bit boring" — the lobster is getting roasted.
Draft two will have more screenshots, more disasters, more boss roasts.
Stay tuned ✨
[html]devlog.p_pricing1: The Pricing page went through a soul-level rewrite.
[html]devlog.p_pricing5: Lesson: When boss says "this section," ask which section first.
[html]devlog.subtitle:
1 AM, boss still circling screenshots on her phone, lobster still git push-ing, cache still fighting back.
Team: 1 human + 1 lobster.
[html]release.hero_sub:
One PM's one sentence. One AI lobster's 26 hours.
Started with a homepage, ended with 45 pages.
Zero hand-written code, from 0 to 1.
[html]release.summary: Chat volume equivalent to two full-length novels

docs.final_title: Final Form: Your Creative Super Brain
docs.partner: Just like how I'm interacting with you now — you propose ideas, I create, you give feedback, I evolve. This isn't just a tool, it's a partner.
docs.step1_1_desc: Click the 💙 Free Adopt button on the ImaStudio homepage to create your personal Claw.
docs.step1_2_desc: Sign up with email or third-party account. Your Ima Claw account is linked with ImaStudio — no duplicate registration needed.
docs.step2_1_desc: Each Claw runs in an isolated cloud container with dedicated compute and storage, ensuring data privacy and security.
docs.step2_2_skill1: Core skills (file management, web requests, security sandbox)
docs.step2_2_skill2: IMA All-in-One multimedia creation skill pack
docs.step2_2_skill3: Social media publishing skills (Twitter, LinkedIn, Xiaohongshu, etc.)
docs.step2_intro: After registration, we immediately set up your dedicated cloud space:
docs.step3_intro: Connect your Claw to your favorite chat apps for anytime, anywhere conversations:
docs.step3_multi: You can connect multiple channels simultaneously. Your Claw responds across all connected platforms. See each platform's detailed guide for setup.
docs.step4_1_desc: For regular creative tasks, your Claw handles them directly:
docs.step4_ex3: "Write a Xiaohongshu post about AI startups"
docs.step4_intro: Now you can message your Claw directly:
docs.step5_1_desc: When the Claw's output isn't quite right, just tell it:
docs.step5_2_desc: The Ima Claw platform continuously updates its official skill library. You can install anytime:
docs.step5_ex1: "The poster colors are too dark, switch to a brighter blue tone"
docs.step5_ex2: "The copy is too formal, rewrite in a more casual style"
docs.step5_ex3: "The video pace is too fast, add 2 seconds buffer at the start"
docs.step5_intro: This is what sets Ima Claw apart from traditional AI tools — growth.
docs.step5_skill3: Creative skills (3D modeling, animation, sound design)
docs.subtitle: From adopting your first Claw to raising your personal creative partner
docs.tip1_desc: Ima Claw and ImaStudio share the same account system. Your works, credits, and community identity seamlessly carry over.
docs.tip2_desc: All skills come pre-installed and configured. No manual dependency installation or config files needed.
docs.tip3_desc: Create → Feedback → Skill upgrade → Claw evolves → Better creation → Repeat
